Re:
Your Request for Advice
our Fi.1e No. A-91- 158
Dear Mr. l.leiss:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Peter F. Schabarun, former Supervisor of Los Angeles County, and
for yourself, treasurer of various comnittees controlled by Mr.
Schabarun, concerning the pernissible use of his campaign funds
under the PoIitical Reform Act (fithe Actr').1
OUEST I ONS
Former Supervisor schabarum Ieft office on l'larch 8, 1991. A
substantial amount of carnpaign funds remains in his carnpaign
committee and in ballot rneasure cornrnittees controlled by him. It
is Mr. schabarum's intention to transfer these campaign funds to a
foundation that he is forning to support political 
causes, such as
ba11ots initiatives. 
This foundation, naned Foundation for Citizen
Representation, wi.ll. be eligible for tax-exempt status under
Internal Revenue Code 501(c) (4).
1. Can former Supervisor Schabarun donate his remaining
canpaign funds to the Foundation for Citizen Representation?
2. If so,
After the canpaign funds are donated to the Foundation
for Citizen RePresentation, the carupaign dollars will be used to
1 Gor"..,*.nt code Sections 81OOO-91015- At1 statutory references
are to the covernnent code unless otherwise indicated. 
cornmission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations. Section
18600, et seo.- AIl references to regulations are to Title 2,
pivision o oi tne caLifornia code of Regulations'
428 J Srreet, Suite St'rO o P.O. Bor 8t17 . Sacranrento C.{ 9 5 fir't-l-t't8!17 o (916) 3:2-;66t-\

58
Page 2
solicit Dore funds for the foundation. For exarople, portions of
the transferred canpaign funds wj.1l be spent on printing, postage
and office Personnel.
a) !.tay the neuly-generated funds obtained through these
solicitation efforts be used to rei.mburse Hr. schabarum for his
actual travel and other exPenses?
b) l,lay the newly-generated funds obtained through these
solicitation efforts be used to pay a salary to Hr. Schabarun
commensurate with his future activities with the foundation?
CONCLUSI ONS
1. It is reasonable to conclude that the proceeds of
campaign funds will have a rrrnaterial financial effect" on the
candidate or elected officer, nenber of his or her imrnediate
f arnily, or carnpaign treasurer if a payment received by hin or her
from the nonprofit organization described in Sections 89515 and
89519 is S25O or more. Thus, Hr. Schabarun's campaign funds may
be donated to the foundation. nrovided that these funds and the
proceeds derived therefron are not used to pay Hr. Schabarum a
salary. reirnburse his expenses, or remit remuneration to hin of
any kind in the anount of 5250 or nore.
2. The fact that canpaign funds nay be used for solicitation
purposes to generate additional donations to the foundation does
not alter the original character or source of the donati.ons in
question, These donations are proceeds derived from Mr.
schabarun's campaign funds. Under the circumstances presented
herein, remuneration or expense reinburseEent to Mr. schabarum by
the foundation would be a direct function of Hr. Schabarutn's
committees' donations to the foundation, and therefore, is
prohibited under the Act.
FACTS
Forner Los Angeles County Supervisor Peter F. Schabarum Left
elected office on !,larch 8, 1991. There are currently in existence
four ca:npaign cornnittees which are controlled by him: Committee
to Retain Supervisor Schabarun (his officeholder campaign
committee), AIliance for Representative Government (originally 
a
general purpose cornnittee subsequently nodified in use to a state
baIlot measure connittee), The Schabarun Pund for CaLifornia,s
Future (a state ballot measure co'n'nittee), and Citizens to Keep
Government Working/Yes on Proposition 140 (a state ball.ot measure
committee) . Robert E. Weiss, Attorney at Law, is treasurer of
these conmittees.
It is the intention of Hr. Schabarurn and l.tr. Weiss to
transfer the funds in these cotrljrittees to a foundation currently
being formed by Hr. Schabarum. This foundation, Foundation for
Citizen Representation, will be a nonprofit, tax-exempt Internal.
Revenue Code S 501 (c) (4) organization. The purpose of the


our File No. A-91-■58
Page 3
foundation is to support political causes, such as baIlot
initiatives and ballot lDeasure coromittees. Three of the five
founding board menbers of the foundation will be ur. Schabarun,
his spouse and his cohDittee treasurer, l{r. weiss.
ANALYSIS
campaign funds under the control of an incudbent candidate
becone surplus funds upon the date on which he or she leaves any
elective office for which the caDPaign funds were raised.
(Section 89519; Regulation 1858?, coly enclosed.3) In
Mr. schabarum's case, the canpaign funds in Connittee To Retain
Supervisor schabaruE became surplus funds on Harch 8, 1991. once
campaign funds become surplus, their use is limited. 
Section
4951g sets forth the penoitted uses of surp).us carnpaign funds.
Subdivj.sion (c) of section 89519 provides that surplus campaign
funds rnay be used to nake:
Id]onations to any bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, re).igious, or sinilar tax-
exeript, nonprofit organization, where no
substantial part of the proceeds wilL have a
r,raterial financial effect on the forner candidate
or elected officer, 
any benber of his or her
irnmediate family, or his or her carnpaign treasurer.
(Enphasis added . )
Thus the Act provides that incumbent candidates who Ieave
office rnay use their surplus campaign funds to rnake donations to
nonprofit organizations provided two requirements are met. First,
the recipient organization of the donation must be a bona fide
charitabl.e, educational, civic, retigious, or si:niIar tax-exempt
nonprofit organization. Second, no substantial part of the
proceeds of the donation rnay have a material financial effect on
the incurnbent candidate who leaves office, rae:nbers of his or her
inrmediate fanily, or his or her conmittee treasurer.
2 comrnittee to Retain Supervisor Schabarun (his officeholder
campaign committee). Based on the facts you have provided,
ALliance for Representative GovernDent nay also falI under this
category. We cannot comhent on past conduct. (Regulation
18329 (b) (8) , copy encl.osed. )
" Regulation 18587 was adopted by the cornmission on Decernber 3,
1990 but has not yet been published. It will take effect in the
near future.
Than Ballot Measure


Our File No. A-9■―■58
Page 4
.rn.tiriate controlted BaIlot Measure Conmittees:{
Sections 89510-89518 set forth the PerDitted uses of canpaign
funds held by a ballot Deasure conmittee. Section 89515 provides:
Campaign funds uay be used to nake donations
or loans to bona fide charitable, educational,
civic, religious, or siurilar tax-exenpt, nonprofit
organizations, where no substantial part of the
proceeds wiIl have a naterial financial effect on
the candidate, elected officer, nenber of his or
her imlrediate fanily, or the caurpaign treasurer,
and wbere the donation or loan bears a reasonable
relation to a political, 
Iegislative, or
governnental PurPose.
(Enphasis added . )
Thus, the Act provides that a ballot neasure committee nay
use its campaign funds to rnake donations to nonprofit
organizations provided three requirenents are net. 
Two of the
reguirements are identical to those contained in the surPlus funds
seition (Secti.on 89519) and are delineated above. The addi'tional
requirenent set forth in Section 89515 is that the donation rnust
beir a reasonable relationshi.P to a po).iticaI, legislative, 
or
qovernnental purpose. (Section 89515, forrnerly nunbered Section
6sso:; see Lee Advice Letter, No. r-90-214, coPy enclosed. )
with regard to the first requirenent, you have represented
that the foundation in guestion. Foundation for Citizen
Representation, is to be forhed as a nonprofit, tax-exenpt
organization, qualifying under S 501(c) (4) of the Internal Revenue
code. You indicated to me that the foundation wiLl be for
politica). purposes only. to encourage and support balIot
initiatives 
and balLot comrnittees. The foundation wil-I educate
citizenry on governnental issues. Accordi.n9ly, we believe that
the foundation neets this first requirement.
with regard to the second reguirernent, you have stated that
Mr. Schabarum, his sPouse, and the connittee treasurer (yourself)
will. be involved in the foundation and that Hr. Schabarum may
benefit financially fron the donations to the foundation. As you
correctly observed in your letter, Sections 89515 and 89519
provide that no substantial part of the proceeds donated to the
tax-exempt organization ruay have a materiaL financial, effect on
the candidate or elected officer who controls the comrnittee, a
mernber of his or her imrnediate family, or the canpaign treasurer.
The intent of these provisions appears to be to prohibit persons
o Th" schabarum Fund for
Government working/Yes on
comrnittees. As discussed
Represen*..at ive Governnent
california,s Future and Citizens to Keep
Proposition 140 are state ba1lot measure
in footnote 3. Alliance for
may or may not faL1 in this category.


Our File NO. A-91-158
Page 5
who control the expenditure of funds by a cornmittee fron
benefiting from these expenditures.
The guestion then presented for our consideration is what
constitutes a "material financial effectI in the context of
perrnissible uses of canpaign funds. while the Coumission has not
defined the tern lroaterial financial ef fectrr in the context of
uses of canpaign funds, it has done so in the context of the Act's
regulation of conflicts of interest. 
General-Iy, these regulations
provide that a transaction has a material financial effect on an
official 
if it increases or decreases his or her personal
expenses, incone, assets or Iiabilities 
by at Least 9250 or nore.
(See Regulation 18?02.1(a) (4); Soain Advice Letter, No. I-90-410;
Statham Advice Letter, I-89-590; Lee Advice Letter, suDra; copies
enclosed. ) Thus, in the absence of a conmission regulation on
this subject, we believe it is reasonable to advise that the
proceeds of campaign funds wiIl have a "ruaterial financial effectr'
on the candidate or elected officer, nernber of his or her
immediate family, or campaign treasurer if a payment received by
him or her fron the nonprofit organization described in Sections
89515 and 89519 is 5250 or nore.
Presumably payment of Hr. Schabarum's salary or expenses from
the proceeds of the donations herein wiII result in an increase or
decrease of at least 5250 in his personal expenses. incorne, assets
or liabilities. 
Therefore, t'tr. schabarun's campaign funds nay not
be donated to the foundation if any Part of the campaign funds
uill be used to pay hirn a salary or reimburse him for expenses.
Put another uay, as discussed above, we current).y are
advising that a Payment of 5250 or more to Mr. Schabarurn, his
spouse, or campaign treasurer from the nonprofit organization in
question, Foundation for Citizen RePresentation, constLtutes a
nraterial financial effect within the meaning of Sections 89515 and
89519. Therefore, Mr. Schabarurn's campaign funds Inay be donated
to the foundation, provided that these funds and the proceeds
derived therefrom are not used to pay IIr. Schabarum, his spouse,
or you as his treasurer, a salary, reimbursernent for exPenses, or
remuneration of any kind of 5250 or more.
The guestion next presented for our consideration is whether
newly-generated funds obtained through solicitation 
efforts may be
used to pay !'tr. schabarum's sal.ary or reinburse his expenses. The
fact that canpaign funds nay be used for solicitation 
purposes to
generate additional donations to the foundation does not alter the
orj.ginal character or source of the donations in question. These
donations are proceeds derived from l1r. Schabarum's carnpaign
funds. Under the circumstances presented herein, renuneration or
expense reimbursenent to Mr. Schabarum by the foundation would be


Our File No. A-9■
―■58
Page 6
a direct function of lIr. SchabaruE's coDEittees' donations to the
foundation, and therefore, is Prohibited under the Act. 
(Seain
Advice Letter, suDra. ) t
In addition, please note it is 1ike1y that payruent of travel
or other expenses or sal,ary for Ur. SchabaruD could result in a
finding that the nonprofit foundation is a controlled connittee of
Mr. schabarum. (Section 82015.) This couLd result if the
payments of expenses or salary were deternined to be contributions
to the candidate and the candidate controlLed the corporation.
(Regulation ]-a2]-7i l,le110 Advice Letter, No. A-91-150, copies
enclosed. ) 
Forner Supervisor Schabaruu continues to be a
"candidatert for purposes of the Act until he terDinates his status
as a cornrnittee pursuant to Section 84214. (Section 82007.)
The third reguirement, ehether the donations bear a
reasonable relationship to a Pol.itj.cal, Iegislative or
governmental purpose, depends on the particular facts of the
donor's situation and only applies to the baIIot neasure
comrnittees controlled by Hr. schabarum. (see Stathan Advice
Letter, supra. ) Here the donations of Mr. Schabaru:n's baLlot
measure conrnittees to the foundation appear to bear a reasonable
relationship to a political 
purpose. rn our telephone
conversation of April 1, 1991, you explained that the primary
purposes of the foundation are to qualj.fy and affect ballot
initiatives 
and to conduct political 
carapaigns in support of such
initiatives. 
(see Spain Advice Letter, supra. )
Gi.ven the above stated statutory requirements and the current
interpretation of "material f inancj.al ef f ectrr discussed above,
donations from any of Hr. schabarum's canpaign or ballot measure
committees to the Foundation for Citizen Representation wiII not
be pernissibfe under the Act if a financial benefit of S25o or
nore f lov/s to Mr. schabarum, his spouse, or his committee
treasurer (yourself) fron these carnpaign funds and the proceeds
derived therefrom. l.Jith regard to state ballot measure comnittees
controlled by Mr. schabarun, we uant to direct your attention to
the trust provisions set forth in the Elections code,
specifically, Elections Code Section 29795 t^rhich places
restrictions on the use of caDpaign funds received by state ball.ot
neasure cornmittees. As a general rule, the Elections Code is
interpreted by the Secretary of state's office and enforced by the
district 
attorney or the Attorney General.'s office. I suggest you
contact them regarding ttr. SchabaruE's and your obligations under
the Elections code.
- Please note, however, that this result does not prevent Hr.
Schabarum, a rnember of his iuunediate fanily, or you frorn receiving
a salary or reirnbursernent fron the foundation. It just requires
that the source of any such reuuneration not be !{r. Schabarun,s
campaign funds or the proceeds therefrou. For this purpose, lre
would reconmend that "independentr donations be segregated fron
campaign funds and the proceeds therefrorn.

Page 7
f trust that this letter has provided you wj'th the guidance
requested. If you have any further guestions regarding this nat-
ter, please contact ne at (916) 322-590L.
SincereLy,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel
